rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical synthesis of functionalized cyclopropanes (tetracyanocyclopropanes and dimethyl 2,2-dicyano-3-arylcyclopropane-1,1-dicarboxylates) from alkylidenemalononitriles and CH-acids using pyridinium bromide (PyHBr) as a redox mediator
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide (from PyHBr) to Br2 acts as the mediator that brominates the CH-acid anion; cathodic reduction of pyridinium cation (PyH+) generates H2 and regenerates free pyridine that deprotonates the CH-acid

key_intermediates.name: free pyridine (from cathodic reduction of PyH+)
pyridinium ylide B (Py+-C(-)(X)(X) from intermediate A and pyridine)
Michael adduct of ylide B with alkylidenemalononitrile leading to cyclopropane via intramolecular displacement of pyridine

electron_flow: At the anode, Br- is oxidized to Br2; at the cathode, PyH+ is reduced to free pyridine and H2. Pyridine deprotonates the CH-acid, the resulting carbanion is brominated by Br2 to give an alpha-bromo CH-acid (A); pyridine displaces bromide to form pyridinium ylide B, which performs Michael addition to the alkylidenemalononitrile followed by intramolecular SN2 expelling pyridine to close the cyclopropane.
notes: Mechanism proposed by analogy with prior chemical synthesis of cyclopropanes from pyridinium ylides and benzylidenemalononitriles (ref 13). No radical traps, KIE, CV, or DFT reported; supporting observations are bromine coloration at the anode and H2 evolution at the cathode. PyHBr acts as both bromide source and proton shuttle/mediator, and the reaction is run in undivided cell with graphite anode/iron cathode in MeCN.
